reasoning: The ICE BofA US Corporate Index Effective Yield had a notable end-of-week value of 4.84 on Friday, November 7, 2025 (which will serve as the benchmark for Sunday, Nov 9). This represented a sharp local spike compared to the 4.67 level seen at the end of October. However, the broader trend from April 2025 (when yields were at 5.38) through October has been strongly downward, aligning with the Federal Reserve's rate-cutting cycle (including another 25 bps cut on Oct 29). The recent spike was largely driven by immediate uncertainty and some strong labor market signals pushing back against expectations of a consecutive December cut. Despite this transient pop, with overall policy easing and QT ending, yields are expected to resume their downward trajectory over the coming months. Therefore, the probability of the yield being strictly higher than 4.84 is below 50% across all dates. Following the directive to increase uncertainty for distant forecasting horizons, the probability estimates approach 0.5 for the later resolution dates.
